CommentBody: Initial triage: Customer reports 50+ users affected. Error message indicates session validation failure. Escalating to Engineering for investigation.
Engineering confirmed: Issue related to v2.5.1 SAML token validation change. Emergency patch being prepared.
Root cause identified: Session token validation was incorrectly configured for SAML assertions with custom attributes. Fix deployed to staging environment.
Patch v2.5.2 deployed to production. Monitoring for any additional issues. Customer confirmed resolution.
Feature request logged. PDF export is on Q2 roadmap (v3.0). Customer added to notification list.
Customer expressed interest in beta testing. Added to beta program waitlist. Closing case as feature request has been acknowledged.
P0 ESCALATION: Healthcare customer with HIPAA requirements. Integration failure affecting patient data availability. Engaging Principal Engineer and healthcare team immediately.
Initial analysis: Connection timeouts correlate with certificate rotation on 2024-01-19. Investigating TLS certificate chain compatibility with Epic systems.
Confirmed: Certificate rotation introduced intermediate CA not recognized by Epic firewall. Working with Epic support to add CA to their trust store.
Performance issue reported. Initial review shows report queries taking 5+ minutes vs expected 30 seconds. Checking database query execution plans.
Database analysis: Missing index on report_data.created_at column causing full table scans. Creating index and monitoring performance improvement.
New case: SSO configuration issue with Azure AD. Customer seeing AADSTS50011 error (Reply URL mismatch). Will review SAML configuration.
License discrepancy confirmed. Order ORD-2024-0125 shows 50 licenses, but provisioning only completed 30. Escalating to License Operations team.
License Operations resolved: Remaining 20 licenses were held for compliance review. Now activated. Customer confirmed all 50 licenses visible.

ESKALATION P1 — EMEA-Kunde TechCorp GmbH meldet vollständigen SSO-Ausfall für ca. 200 Benutzer in München. Ursache: Tokenvalidierungsfehler nach Plattform-Update 4.2.1 (AADSTS70011). Engineering-Team informiert, Notfall-Rollback in Vorbereitung. Zuständiger Engineer: Thomas Reiter.

Root cause confirmed: Version 4.2.1 introduced stricter OAuth scope validation incompatible with certain Azure AD tenant configurations (scope parameter format change). Configuration rollback deployed at 13:40 UTC. SSO restored for all affected users. Incident report to follow within 24h. Permanent fix targeted for version 4.2.2.
